source.title: 180+ Test Cases Example for Web Application Testing
source.author: Vijay

execution_notes: Execute these scenarios with different user roles such as admin users and guest users.
For web applications, test across approved browsers such as IE, Firefox, Chrome, and Safari.
Test with different screen resolutions such as 1024x768 and 1280x1024.
Test on different displays including notebooks, tablets, and mobile phones.
Test on different operating systems such as Windows, Mac, and Linux.
assumptions: Search filter criteria and result grids

categories.title: General Test Scenarios
items: All mandatory fields should be validated and indicated by an asterisk symbol.
Validation error messages should be displayed properly and in the correct position.
All error messages should be displayed in the same CSS style.
General confirmation messages should use CSS style different from error message style.
Tooltip text should be meaningful.
Drop-down fields should have the first entry as blank or text like Select.
Delete functionality for any record should ask for confirmation.
Select or deselect all records option should be provided if the page supports record add, delete, or update.
Amount values should be displayed with the correct currency symbols.
Default page sorting should be provided.
Reset button functionality should set default values for all fields.
All numeric values should be formatted properly.
Input fields should enforce the max field value and reject values above the limit.
All input fields should be checked for special characters.
Field labels should be standard and meaningful.
Page sorting should work correctly after add, edit, and delete operations.
Timeout functionality should be checked and timeout values should be configurable.
Cookies used in the application should be checked.
Downloadable files should point to the correct file path.
All resource keys should be configurable instead of hard coded.
Standard naming conventions should be followed for resource keys.
Markup for all pages should comply with standards.
Crashes or unavailable pages should redirect to an error page.
Text on all pages should be checked for spelling and grammar.
Numeric fields should reject character input with a proper validation message.
Negative number handling should be checked where applicable.
Decimal number fields should be verified.
Functionality of buttons on all pages should be checked.
The user should not be able to submit the page twice by pressing submit quickly.
Divide by zero errors should be handled for calculations.
Input data with leading and trailing blanks should be handled correctly.

categories.title: GUI and Usability Test Scenarios
items: All fields on the page should be aligned properly.
Numeric values should be justified correctly unless specified otherwise.
Enough space should exist between labels, rows, columns, and error messages.
Scrollbars should be enabled only when necessary.
Font size, style, and color should be standard as specified in requirements.
Description text boxes should support multiple lines.
Disabled fields should be greyed out and should not accept focus.
Mouse pointer should change to cursor for input text fields.
The user should not be able to type into non-editable drop-down select lists.
Information entered by the user should remain intact after validation errors.
Proper field labels should be used in error messages.
Drop-down values should appear in the defined sort order.
Tab and Shift+Tab order should work properly.
Default radio options should be preselected on page load.
Field-specific and page-level help messages should be available.
Correct fields should be highlighted when errors occur.
Drop-down list options should be readable and not truncated.
All buttons should be accessible with keyboard shortcuts where applicable.
All pages should be checked for broken images.
All pages should be checked for broken links.
All pages should have a title.
Confirmation messages should appear before updates or deletes.
A busy indicator should be displayed when the application is processing.
Page text should be left-justified.
Only one radio option and valid checkbox combinations should be selectable.

categories.title: Test Scenarios for Filter Criteria
items: The user should be able to filter results using all parameters on the page.
Refine search functionality should load the search page with all selected parameters.
If at least one filter is required, submitting without a filter should show a proper error.
If filter selection is optional, the page should submit with default search criteria.
Proper validation messages should be displayed for invalid filter values.

categories.title: Test Scenarios for Result Grid
items: A page loading symbol should be displayed when result loading takes longer than the default time.
All search parameters should be used to fetch the result data.
The total number of results should be displayed in the result grid.
Search criteria used for searching should be displayed in the result grid.
Result grid values should be sorted by the default column.
Sorted columns should display a sort icon.
Result grids should include all specified columns with correct values.
Ascending and descending sort should work for sortable columns.
Result grids should have proper column and row spacing.
Pagination should be enabled when results exceed the default page size.
Next, Previous, First, and Last pagination should work.
Duplicate records should not be displayed in the results grid.
All columns should be visible and horizontal scrollbars should appear if needed.
Dynamic column values should be verified.
Totals row should be checked for report grids.
Totals row should remain correct when pagination is enabled.
Proper symbols should be used for column values such as percentages.
Result grid data should be checked when date range filtering is enabled.

categories.title: Test Scenarios for a Window
items: Default window size should be correct.
Child window size should be correct.
Default focus should be set to the correct field.
Child windows should close when the parent or opener window is closed.
The user should not be able to use the parent window while a modal child window is open.
Minimize, maximize, and close functions should work.
Window resize should work if supported.
Scrollbars should work properly for parent and child windows.
Cancel button functionality should work for child windows.

categories.title: Database Testing Test Scenarios
items: Correct data should be saved in the database after successful page submit.
Values for columns not accepting null should be verified.
Data integrity should be maintained across tables according to design.
Index names should follow naming standards.
Tables should have a primary key column.
Table columns should have description information where required.
Database add and update operations should create logs.
Required table indexes should exist.
Data should be committed only when the operation completes successfully.
Data should be rolled back when transactions fail.
Database names should follow environment naming conventions.
Logical database names should follow standards.
Stored procedures should not use reserved prefixes such as sp_.
Audit columns should be populated correctly.
Input data should not be truncated while saving.
Numeric fields should be checked with minimum, maximum, and float values.
Negative numeric values should be checked for both acceptance and rejection.
Radio button and drop-down values should be saved correctly.
Database fields should use the correct data type and length.
Primary key, foreign key, and other constraints should be implemented correctly.
Stored procedures and triggers should be tested with sample input data.
Leading and trailing spaces should be trimmed before committing to the database.
Null values should not be allowed for primary key columns.

categories.title: Test Scenarios for Image Upload Functionality
items: Uploaded image path should be checked.
Image upload and change functionality should work.
Image upload should work with supported image extensions.
Image upload should work with filenames containing spaces or allowed special characters.
Duplicate image name upload should be checked.
Images larger than the max allowed size should be rejected with an error message.
Non-image files should be rejected with an error message.
Specified height and width constraints should be enforced if defined.
Upload progress bar should appear for large images.
Cancel button should work during upload.
File selection dialog should show only supported files where applicable.
Multiple image upload functionality should work if supported.
Image quality should remain acceptable after upload.
Uploaded images should be viewable and usable by the user.

categories.title: Test Scenarios for Sending Emails
items: Email templates should use standard CSS.
Email addresses should be validated before sending.
Special characters in email body templates should be handled properly.
Language-specific characters should be handled properly in email bodies.
Email subject should not be blank.
Placeholder fields in templates should be replaced with actual values.
Dynamic report values included in emails should be calculated correctly.
Sender name should not be blank.
Emails should be checked in multiple email clients.
TO, CC, and BCC should work correctly.
Plain text emails should be checked.
HTML format emails should be checked.
Email header and footer should include correct branding and links.
Emails with attachments should work correctly.
Sending to single, multiple, and distribution list recipients should work.
Reply-to address should be correct.
High volume email sending should be checked.

categories.title: Test Scenarios for Excel Export Functionality
items: Exported files should use the proper file extension.
Exported filenames should follow standards and replace timestamps correctly.
Date formats should be correct in exported files.
Numeric and currency formatting should match the page display.
Exported files should contain proper column names.
Default page sorting should be reflected in the export.
Exported data should include proper header, footer, date, and page number formatting.
Displayed page data and exported file data should match.
Export functionality should work correctly when pagination is enabled.
Export buttons should show the proper icon for the file type.
Export functionality should work for very large files.
Special characters should be exported correctly.

categories.title: Performance Testing Test Scenarios
items: Page load time should be within acceptable range.
Pages should load correctly on slow connections.
Response time for actions should be checked under different load conditions.
Performance of database stored procedures and triggers should be checked.
Database query execution time should be checked.
Load testing of the application should be performed.
Stress testing of the application should be performed.
CPU and memory usage under peak load should be monitored.

categories.title: Security Testing Test Scenarios
items: Check for SQL injection attacks.
Secure pages should use HTTPS.
Application crashes should not reveal application or server information.
Special characters in input should be escaped.
Error messages should not reveal sensitive information.
Credentials should be transferred over encrypted channels.
Password security and policy enforcement should be checked.
Logout functionality should be checked.
Brute force attack resistance should be checked.
Cookie information should be stored in encrypted format.
Session cookie duration and termination after timeout or logout should be checked.
Session tokens should be transmitted over secure channels.
Passwords should not be stored in cookies.
Denial of Service resistance should be considered.
Memory leakage should be checked.
Unauthorized access by manipulating address bar variables should be checked.
File extension handling should prevent dangerous executables from being uploaded or run.
Sensitive fields should not use unsafe autocomplete settings.
File uploads should use file type restrictions and antivirus scanning.
Directory listing should be prohibited.
Passwords and sensitive fields should be masked while typing.
Forgot password should be secured with expiry and verification steps.
CAPTCHA functionality should be verified.
Important events should be logged.
Access privileges should be implemented correctly.
